title: Send a Clear Text Message
summary: Write a text message that is clear, polite, and easy to understand.
learnerPromise: You will be able to send text messages that get your point across without confusion.
whyItMatters: Most everyday communication happens through texting. Clear messages save time, reduce misunderstandings, and help you come across as respectful and competent.
realLifeUses: Texting a friend to confirm plans for the weekend
Messaging a coworker about a shift change
Asking a teacher a quick question about an assignment
Letting a family member know you are running late
materialsNeeded: A phone or device with messaging ability
result: A clear, polite text message sent to someone
youWillLearn: Open a message with a greeting or context
State the main point in one or two sentences
Use punctuation and line breaks for clarity
Close with a polite sign-off when appropriate
miniChallenge: Send one text message to someone you know using a greeting, a clear point, and a closing line.
steps: Decide who you are messaging and what you need to say
Write a short greeting or opening line
State your main point in one or two sentences
Read the message out loud to check for clarity
completionCriteria: The message includes a greeting, a clear point, and a polite closing
The recipient understands what you are saying without needing to ask follow-up questions
You sent the message within 10 minutes
commonProblems: Trying to say too much in one message
Using slang or abbreviations the other person may not know
Forgetting to include important details like a time or place
tips: If you have several points, use line breaks or send separate messages
Reread your message before sending to catch typos or unclear wording
Match your tone to the person you are texting
